title: Orderability and the Contact Hofer Norm
speaker: Jakob Hedicke
abstract:
In this talk we discuss a relation between the contact version 
of the Hofer norm and positive loops of contactomorphisms.
This leads to a new criterion for the existence of contractible positive 
loops in terms of open book decompositions and previously unknown 
examples of non-orderable contact manifolds.
The talk is based on joint work with Egor Shelukhin.

proof_stages.label: Contact positivity and orderability
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes a closed cooriented contact manifold, defines positive/nonnegative paths of contactomorphisms by their contact Hamiltonians, uses them to define a bi-invariant relation on the universal cover, and recalls that orderability is equivalent to the nonexistence of a positive contractible loop.

proof_stages.label: Stabilized Liouville boundaries and the main theorem
proof_stages.summary: After recalling the Eliashberg-Kim-Polterovich nonorderability theorem for ideal boundaries of two-stabilizations, the speaker states the new result that one-stabilizations of Weinstein manifolds of dimension at least four, plus the S^2 x S^1 low-dimensional case, are nonorderable.

proof_stages.label: Contact Hofer pseudo-norm as the obstruction detector
proof_stages.summary: The contact Hofer length and norm are defined, their naturality under conjugation is emphasized, and orderability is translated into linear growth of the lifted Reeb flow norm, so a uniform bound on that norm will prove nonorderability.

proof_stages.label: Open-book model for one-stabilizations
proof_stages.summary: Supporting open books with Weinstein pages are introduced, and the ideal boundary of W x C is identified with its canonical open book whose pages are exact symplectomorphic to W.

proof_stages.label: Skeleton-complement Hofer bound
proof_stages.summary: For the skeleton L of a page in such an open book, the speaker states and explains the key lemma that contactomorphism paths compactly supported in M\L have uniformly bounded contact Hofer norm, using an open-book contraction flow and naturality of the norm.

proof_stages.label: Fragmentation reduction to avoiding two skeleta
proof_stages.summary: A fragmentation lemma splits a contact isotopy into factors supported away from two sets whenever one set avoids the image of the other, reducing the Reeb-flow bound to perturbing the Reeb path so that one page skeleton never meets another.

proof_stages.label: Subcritical avoidance and conclusion
proof_stages.summary: When W is subcritical, dimension counting and a Hofer-small generic perturbation make the Reeb image of one skeleton avoid the other, so fragmentation and the skeleton-complement bound yield a uniform Reeb-flow norm bound and hence nonorderability.

proof_stages.label: Critical case via loose Legendrians
proof_stages.summary: In the critical Weinstein case, the critical parts of the two skeleta are loose Legendrians, and Nakamura's Hofer bound for paths of loose Legendrians supplies a uniformly bounded correction that completes the same Reeb-flow norm estimate.
